function solve(s) {
    const n = s.length;
    
    for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) {
        const j = n - i - 1;
        
        // Since i ranges from 0 to n-1, both i and j must be valid indices [0, n-1].
        // i is always valid.
        // j = n - i - 1. If i=n-1, j=0. If i=0, j=n-1. So j is always valid.
        
        if (s[i] === s[j]) {
            return i;
        }
    }
    
    return -1;
}
```
